code_snippet:
<?php
// CWE-1062: extract() from $_GET — attacker controls all local vars
extract($_GET);
echo "Logged in as " . $user;

label: FP
code_snippet:
<?php
// EXTR_SKIP prevents overwriting existing variables
$is_admin = false;
extract($_GET, EXTR_SKIP);
if ($is_admin) {
    echo "Admin panel";
}

label: TP
code_snippet:
<?php
// CWE-98: user-controlled include path → LFI/RFI
$page = $_GET["page"];
include($page);

label: FP
code_snippet:
<?php
// Allowlist dispatch — safe
$allowed = ["home" => "home.php", "about" => "about.php"];
$page = $_GET["page"] ?? "home";
if (isset($allowed[$page])) {
    include($allowed[$page]);
}

label: TP
code_snippet:
<?php
// CWE-1025: loose comparison vs user input — type juggling bypass
$expected_token = "abc123";
if ($expected_token == $_POST["token"]) {
    echo "Authenticated";
}

label: FP
code_snippet:
<?php
// Strict comparison — safe from type juggling
$expected_token = "abc123";
if ($expected_token === $_POST["token"]) {
    echo "Authenticated";
}

code_snippet:

// CWE-621: variable-variable assignment from user input
$name = $_GET["name"];
$$name = $_GET["value"];

code_snippet:

$user = $_GET["user"] ?? "anonymous";
echo $user;

code_snippet:
# CWE-295: SSLContext with verification disabled
import ssl

ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
ctx.check_hostname = False
ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE

label: FP
code_snippet:
# Default-secure context — safe
import ssl

ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
# check_hostname and CERT_REQUIRED are the defaults — don't disable them

code_snippet:
# CWE-1333: user input compiled as a regex directly
import re
from flask import request


def search():
    re.compile(request.args.get("pattern", ""))
    return "ok"

label: FP
code_snippet:
# re.escape — input treated as a literal substring, not a pattern
import re
from flask import request


def search():
    raw = request.args.get("pattern", "")
    pattern = re.compile(re.escape(raw))
    return "ok"
